question.text: A listed company with 1.5 lakh shareholders manages its share transfer activities in-house. Under SEBI LODR 2015, what is the company required to do?

answer.text: Continue in-house operations without any additional registration
Register as a Category I RTA with SEBI
Register as a Category II Share Transfer Agent with SEBI
Outsource to an external SEBI-registered RTA within 90 days

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI LODR, if a listed entity handles share transfer activities in-house and has more than 1 lakh security holders, it must register as a Category II Share Transfer Agent with SEBI. Category II covers entities that perform only the transfer agent function (not full registrar services). **The Trap:** Category I covers both registrar and transfer agent functions combined. The 1 lakh threshold is the trigger point -- below this, in-house handling without separate registration may be acceptable. **Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI RTA Regulations 1993 define Category I (registrar + transfer agent) and Category II (registrar or transfer agent). LODR connects this to the listed entity's obligations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Most large listed companies outsource to external RTAs to avoid the compliance burden of Category II registration. But smaller companies near the 1 lakh threshold should be advised to plan ahead for this requirement.
